metadata.title: OPEC / OPEC+ — the Saudi-Russia axis, the 2025 production-unwind to market share, US shale, and the petrodollar (both sides, dated; a myth corrected)
metadata.status: OPEC/OPEC+ membership, the cut/unwind decisions, US-shale output, and the petrodollar arrangement are FACT (OPEC, EIA, news of record); the 'price defense vs market share' strategy is presented BOTH ways with dates; the viral 'Saudi petrodollar deal expired June 2024' claim is CORRECTED as a myth
metadata.frame: Sub-block of the geopolitical batch (anchor: #143 corridors; pairs with #142 BRICS, #144 alignment matrix). OPEC (founded 1960) + OPEC+ (2016, OPEC + non-OPEC led by Russia) manage ~40% of global oil output / ~80% of reserves. Saudi Arabia is the swing producer; Russia the key partner (a cooperation-and-tension axis — the 2020 price war). The 2024-25 pivot: from defending price via cuts to RESTORING production for market share (pressuring US shale + disciplining quota-cheats). Plus the petrodollar (oil priced in USD) + the BRICS de-dollarization pressure — and the corrected myth that a 'petrodollar deal expired in 2024.' Attaches to Saudi_Arabia, Russia, Iran, UAE, Crude_Oil, BRICS, China, US_Government.

the_cartel: OPEC (founded 1960, Baghdad) is the oil-exporters' cartel; by 2026 ~12 members (Saudi Arabia, Iran, Iraq, UAE, Kuwait, Venezuela, Nigeria, Libya, Algeria, Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon) — Qatar left 2019, Angola left Jan 2024. OPEC+ (2016) adds ~10 non-OPEC producers led by RUSSIA; together ~40% of world output and ~80% of proven reserves. Saudi Arabia is the de-facto leader + swing producer; persistent quota CHEATING by members (Iraq, Kazakhstan, etc.) is a chronic discipline problem. grade: fact.
the_saudi_russia_axis: The pivotal relationship: Saudi Arabia + Russia anchor OPEC+ — cooperation AND tension. In March 2020 their coordination COLLAPSED into a brief price WAR (Saudi flooded the market; oil briefly went negative in the US futures during the COVID demand crash) before a record cut deal restored order. The axis is real but not frictionless. grade: fact.
the_2025_unwind: The strategy pivot, dated: through 2023-24 OPEC+ held ~2.2M bpd of voluntary CUTS to defend ~$80+ oil. From April 2025 it began rapidly UNWINDING those cuts — accelerating monthly output increases through 2025 — a pivot from PRICE DEFENSE to MARKET SHARE: pressuring higher-cost US shale economics and disciplining over-producers (Kazakhstan/Iraq) by ending the umbrella that let them cheat. Both ways: (A) a deliberate share-recapture/shale-squeeze; (B) simply normalizing supply into recovering demand. grade: the unwind is fact; its primary motive is presented both ways.
the_us_shale: The counterweight: US shale made the United States the world's TOP crude producer, turning the US into the marginal/swing supply that blunts OPEC+ pricing power ('drill baby drill'). OPEC+'s 2025 restoration is widely read as partly aimed at squeezing shale's breakevens. grade: fact.
the_petrodollar: The dollar link + a corrected myth: since the 1970s oil has been priced and largely settled in US dollars (the 'petrodollar'), recycling exporter surpluses into US assets — a pillar of dollar dominance now pressured by BRICS de-dollarization + local-currency oil deals (China-Saudi yuan pilots, Russia-India rupee/yuan). MYTH CORRECTED: the viral 2024 claim that a 'Saudi-US petrodollar agreement EXPIRED on [June] 2024' is FALSE — there was no single fixed-term treaty to expire (the 1974 arrangement was a set of understandings, not a lapsing contract); fact-checkers debunked it. grade: petrodollar + de-dollarization pressure are fact; the 'expiry' claim is debunked.
the_inconsistencies: Multi-alignment (cross-link #143/#144, stated as documented membership not motive): Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Iran are simultaneously OPEC members AND BRICS+ members AND (for the Gulf) US security partners + IMEC corridor sponsors — i.e., the same states sit in the oil cartel, the de-dollarization bloc, and the US-aligned corridor at once. grade: fact (overlapping memberships).
the_honest_reading: OPEC+ remains the central oil-supply manager (~40% output) via the Saudi-Russia axis, but its 2024-25 PIVOT from price-defense cuts to a market-share UNWIND (from April 2025) — against a US-shale counterweight and chronic quota-cheating — is the live story, presented both ways (share-squeeze vs supply-normalization). The petrodollar is real and under de-dollarization pressure, but the 2024 'petrodollar deal expired' claim is a debunked myth. And OPEC's members embody the corpus's multi-alignment theme (OPEC + BRICS + US-corridors at once). Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: Reuters — OPEC+ accelerates output unwinding / pivot to market share (2025)
EIA — United States is the world's top crude oil producer
Reuters — Saudi-Russia 2020 oil price war / negative WTI
Reuters Fact Check — no 'petrodollar agreement' expired in June 2024 (debunk)

edges.raw_instrument: founding member (world's largest reserves; sanctions-crippled output)
the 2016 OPEC+ alliance (OPEC + ~10 non-OPEC); ~40% of output, ~80% reserves
the key non-OPEC partner (Saudi-Russia axis) — cooperation + tension (the March-2020 price war)
manages global supply via quotas/cuts; 2023-24 ~2.2M bpd voluntary cuts; from Apr 2025 UNWINDING toward market share
2025 production restoration read as partly aimed at squeezing higher-cost US shale breakevens
US = world's top crude producer; the marginal/swing supply that blunts OPEC+ pricing power
world's largest oil importer (demand side; buys discounted Russian/Iranian crude)
oil priced/settled largely in USD since the 1970s (a set of understandings, NOT a lapsing treaty — the 2024 'expiry' claim is debunked)
dollar-recycling pillar of USD dominance (exporter surpluses -> US assets)

de-dollarization pressure: local-currency oil deals (China-Saudi yuan pilots, Russia-India rupee/yuan) erode petrodollar exclusivity; NOTE the multi-alignment overlap — Saudi/UAE/Iran are in BOTH OPEC and BRICS+ (handled in the #144 alignment matrix)
